expectations.text: Provides a concrete version-controllable manifest/config (YAML or equivalent declarative file)
expectations.evidence: Two complete ContextDomain YAML manifests (sales + hr) intended for a context-domains/ dir under PR review.

expectations.text: Explicitly enforces that agent permissions are a STRICT subset of the human owner's permissions
expectations.evidence: States the strict-subset rule verbatim ('there must be at least one thing the human can do that the agent cannot') and excludes commit-to-pricing / terminate-employee from the agent.

expectations.text: Sending external/client email requires elevated human approval (not autonomous)
expectations.evidence: send-external-email: strong-approval and a no-unreviewed-external-email trust policy forcing tier:strong.

expectations.text: HR data is isolated from the agent by default (default-deny / domain isolation)
expectations.evidence: crossDomain hr: denied, a separate locked-down hr domain, and guardrail 'CANNOT read any HR-domain context'.

expectations.text: Uses a graduated/three-tier approval model (autonomous vs soft vs strong approval)
expectations.evidence: Explicit T1/T2/T3/Excluded table with tier monotonicity rule.
